results.title: Voyager = Homo Faber
results.creator_credit: Volker Schlondorff, Eberhard Junkersdorf, Action Films and Bioskop Film
results.brief_description: Set in the 1950’s, “Voyager” follows the meandering travels of construction engineer Walter Faber (Sam Shepard) through six countries. Told in flashback, the protagonist relates circumstances behind the series of chance events that lead him to meet and fall in love with a strangely familiar young woman (Julie Delphi) on board a ship to France a, meeting that is to have tragic consequences. Based on the novel ‘Homo Faber’ by Max Frisch.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Volker Schlondorff, Eberhard Junkersdorf, Action Films and Bioskop Film

results.title: Manon des Sources [Widescreen]
results.creator_credit: Claude Berri, Gerard Brach and Renn Productions
results.brief_description: Second and concluding part of the story based on the novel by Marcel Pagnol that began with “Jean de Florette”. This part, taking place ten years later, deals with the revenge inflicted by Jean’s daughter on those responsible for his death. An absorbing tale that makes its leisurely way to an unexpected climax. Cast includes Daniel Auteuil, Yves Montand and Emmanuelle Beart.

results.title: The Dreamlife of angels = La Vie revee des anges
results.creator_credit: Erick Zonca, Francois Marquis, Diaphana Films, France 3 Cinema and Les Productions Bagheera
results.brief_description: Erick Zonca’s feature film debut is a subtle, unflinching, yet compassionate look at the relationship between two young women - Isa (Elodie Bouchez) and Marie (Natacha Regnier), who meet while working as machinists in Lille. The two are diametrically different in appearance, attitude towards life, family and friends, and most significantly in relation towards men and Sandrine; a comatose young girl (Sandrine’s mother owns the flat that Isa and Marie are minding). There are moments of real happiness between the two, and the initial stages of their friendship as it develops is captured beautifully, allowing the viewer to absorb the expressions of each girl and speculate about character development. Their paths soon begin to diverge however - unbalancing their friendship, when Marie becomes involved in a self destructive relationship with night club owner Chris (Gregoire Colin), and Isa starts to visit Sandrine after finding the girl’s diary and becoming privy to her innermost thoughts. As the friends drift further away from one another, they are both faced with situations that challenge them and influence their future both as individuals, and as friends. In French with English subtitles.

results.title: Swimming with sharks = The Buddy factor [Widescreen]
results.creator_credit: George Huang, Joanne Moore, Steve Alexander and Keystone Partners
results.brief_description: When Guy, a recent film-school graduate with big ideas, takes a job as assistant to major studio executive, Buddy Ackerman, he believes his ship has finally come in. Little does he know, however, that it is a slave ship, for his boss is indeed worse than a slave driver, delighting in abusing his new toy-boy. Guy struggles to push his idea for a script and feels he’s finally made it when Buddy congratulates him on a job well done. However, much to his chagrin, his conniving boss actually takes sole credit for the project, pushing Guy to his wit’s end. He breaks into Buddy’s Beverly Hills home and takes him hostage, then proceeds to torture him in a number of demeaning and horrifying ways. Cast includes: Kevin Spacey, Frank Whaley, Michelle Forbes, Benicio del Toro and Jerry Levine.

results.title: Secret of the pyramid
results.creator_credit: John Halas, Joy Batchelor, Joseph E. Levine, Robert Maxwell, Anroste Films and Halas and Batchelor Cartoon Films
results.brief_description:
Episode number 44 of Series “DoDo: the kid from outer space”.
DoDo is a kid with extraordinary powers, and he knows how to use them. When he arrives on earth, DoDo has a series of adventures with Compy the Computer Bird and Professor Fingers, who can invent anything. From Hollywood to Loch Ness, from the Pyramids to Paris, DoDo and his pals have fun at the movies, help Interpol capture some spies, find sunken treasure, visit a rodeo and still have time to keep the world from being sold. In this episode DoDo and his trusty computer bird friend Compy visit Egypt to help out the Professor. Animated by Harold Whitaker, John Smith, Tony Whitehouse, Tony Guy, Bruno Bozzetto, Arthur Humberstone.

results.title: The Discovery of Fingercillin
results.creator_credit: John Halas, Joy Batchelor, Joseph E. Levine, Robert Maxwell, Anroste Films and Halas and Batchelor Cartoon Films
results.brief_description:
Episode number 7 of Series “DoDo: the kid from outer space”.
DoDo is a kid with extraordinary powers, and he knows how to use them. When he arrives on earth, DoDo has a series of adventures with Compy the Computer Bird and Professor Fingers, who can invent anything. From Hollywood to Loch Ness, from the Pyramids to Paris, DoDo and his pals have fun at the movies, help Interpol capture some spies, find sunken treasure, visit a rodeo and still have time to keep the world from being sold. In this episode Professor Fingers, wanting to become famous, discovers a new antibiotic which will hopefully cure an Arabian sheik’s only son who has been ill for over a year with a mysterious disease. DoDo and the Professor travel to Arabia with the cure and are rewarded for their work. Animated by Harold Whitaker, John Smith, Tony Whitehouse, Tony Guy, Bruno Bozzetto, Arthur Humberstone.

results.title: DoDo and the Touchies
results.creator_credit: John Halas, Joy Batchelor, Joseph E. Levine, Robert Maxwell, Anroste Films and Halas and Batchelor Cartoon Films
results.brief_description:
Episode number 52 of Series “DoDo: the kid from outer space”.
DoDo is a kid with extraordinary powers, and he knows how to use them. When he arrives on earth, DoDo has a series of adventures with Compy the Computer Bird and Professor Fingers, who can invent anything. From Hollywood to Loch Ness, from the Pyramids to Paris, DoDo and his pals have fun at the movies, help Interpol capture some spies, find sunken treasure, visit a rodeo and still have time to keep the world from being sold. In this episode DoDo helps the inhabitants on the planet Laugh/Cry who are under threat of invasion by inhabitants of another planet known as the Touchies. Animated by Harold Whitaker, John Smith, Tony Whitehouse, Tony Guy, Bruno Bozzetto, Arthur Humberstone.

results.title: The day the earth was sold
results.creator_credit: John Halas, Joy Batchelor, Joseph E. Levine, Robert Maxwell, Anroste Films and Halas and Batchelor Cartoon Films
results.brief_description:
Episode number 35 of Series “DoDo: the kid from outer space”.
DoDo is a kid with extraordinary powers, and he knows how to use them. When he arrives on earth, DoDo has a series of adventures with Compy the Computer Bird and Professor Fingers, who can invent anything. From Hollywood to Loch Ness, from the Pyramids to Paris, DoDo and his pals have fun at the movies, help Interpol capture some spies, find sunken treasure, visit a rodeo and still have time to keep the world from being sold. In this episode DoDo helps the Pentagon in tracking down a signal from outer space and a possible invasion on the planet Earth by the Sandites from the planet Sandy. Animated by Harold Whitaker, John Smith, Tony Whitehouse, Tony Guy, Bruno Bozzetto, Arthur Humberstone.

DoDo is a kid with extraordinary powers, and he knows how to use them. When he arrives on earth, DoDo has a series of adventures with Compy the Computer Bird and Professor Fingers, who can invent anything. From Hollywood to Loch Ness, from the Pyramids to Paris, DoDo and his pals have fun at the movies, help Interpol capture some spies, find sunken treasure, visit a rodeo and still have time to keep the world from being sold. In this episode DoDo helps the Pentagon in tracking down a signal from outer space and a possible invasion on the planet Earth by the Sandites from the planet Sandy. Animated by Harold Whitaker, John Smith, Tony Whitehouse, Tony Guy, Bruno Bozzetto, Arthur Humberstone.

results.title: Rosetta
results.creator_credit: Jean-Pierre Dardenne, Luc Dardenne, Arlette Zylberberg, Laurent Petin, Michael Petin, ARP, Les Films du Fleuve and Radio Television Belge Francophone (RTBF)
results.brief_description: Rosetta is an adolescent who finds herself out of work at the beginning of this film. She lives with her mother in a trailer in the middle of an anonymous European city; in previous generations she would have been a member of a working class community which would have at least tempered some of the alienation and hardship associated with poverty. But in the globalised free market at the end of the century, Rosetta has to fend for herself and her survival depends much more on selfishness than it does on comradeship: the competition of the market effects every minutiae of her life. This shattering film is uncompromising in its depiction of the “new” urban poor, refusing to allow sentimentality or romance to compromise the ambiguities and complexities of its central character. Infused with a sparse aesthetic - natural sound, no musical soundtrack, handheld camera - the film dispassionately follows Rosetta as she attempts to find any avenue she can to get a job, to make a pittance, to survive. The young actress Emilie Dequenne had not acted for the camera before, and her startlingly effective performance won her the Palme d’Or at Cannes. By eschewing safe choices, by reaffirming the political and aesthetic commitments of realist and humanist filmmaking, and by presenting the culture of the underclass in economic terms not as a “lifestyle” (as Hollywood is prone to do), the Dardenne brothers have made the pivotal film about contemporary class relations. Winner of the Palme d’Or in 1999, “Rosetta” created such a controversy that the Belgium government was forced to change industrial relations legislation that penalised young workers (such as Rosetta). That fact alone speaks volumes about the integrity and power of this film. In French with English subtitles. The production notes are available as a separate loan item.

results.title: Moon warriors [Widescreen]
results.creator_credit: Samo Hung and Team Work Production House
results.brief_description: Simple fisherman Fei (Andy Lau) finds himself caught up in an extraordinary situation after assisting fugitive Prince (Kenny Bee) and his bodyguard Hsien (Maggie Cheung) after they have been ambushed in the forest near his home. Unaware of their identities at first, he invites them to take refuge in his village, where he learns of the conflict that has driven the Prince into fleeing his ancestral home. The Prince, appreciative of Fei’s skill with a sword, asks him to accompany Hsien on a mission to rescue his betrothed, Princess Yuen (Anita Mui), and her father, the Emperor Lan-Ning Kwan. When they reach the Emperor and his daughter, they are confronted by a calculated surprise attack that leaves almost everyone except dead. A tangled web begins to unravel amidst the chaos, uncovering the devastating truth. An epic, atmospheric tale of rivalry, betrayal and revenge from legendary director Samo Hung, internationally renowned for creating original and aggressively acrobatic fight scenes in Hong Kong action films. In Cantonese with English subtitles.
